episode_key: 2024-10-28 - How to make $20,000,000 with a landing page
title: How to make $20,000,000 with a landing page
executive_summary: Jonathan Courtney, CEO of AJ&Smart, reveals how a single ugly funnel generated over $20M in revenue across 3.5 years, demonstrating that conversion-optimized funnels dramatically outperform polished websites. The episode contrasts websites (1-3% conversion) with funnels (10-25% conversion) and introduces the MIFGE (Most Incredible Free Gift Ever) strategy used by brands like AG1 and Eight Sleep. Key insight: funnels and websites should be completely separate properties, with funnels focused exclusively on one CTA.
key_takeaways: Build dedicated funnels separate from your website - ugly, copy-heavy funnels consistently outperform beautiful branded pages for revenue generation
Use the MIFGE (Most Incredible Free Gift Ever) strategy to stack one-time bonuses onto offers, dramatically increasing conversion rates even for high-ticket products
Focus your entire content team on driving traffic to one funnel with one goal (e.g., call bookings), rather than spreading across multiple destinations
opportunities: VSL (Video Sales Letter) funnel agency for bootstrapped businesses selling high-ticket services ($7K-$14K range)
Book funnel as lead generation - physical books drive disproportionate engagement and user-generated marketing content
quotes: If you aren't disgusted with your funnel, you've launched too late

episode_key: 2024-10-30 - How to steal funnels that crush
title: How to steal funnels that crush
executive_summary: Jordan, operating partner at Late Checkout, teaches how to build a 'swipe file' - a curated vault of proven marketing winners that transforms passive content consumption into future revenue. The episode walks through real examples of scroll-stopping creative (fried chicken ice cream, Colin & Samir thumbnails) and IRL-to-URL lead magnets, demonstrating how the best marketers systematically study how they get sold. A concrete micro-SaaS idea emerges: an AI agent that scrapes and catalogs entire marketing funnels automatically.
key_takeaways: Build a systematic swipe file using tools like MyMind - tag everything that 'got you' (made you click, subscribe, or buy) to turn consumption into curation
Study how you personally get sold to understand conversion psychology - the best way to learn selling is analyzing what worked on you
In your funnel, top-of-funnel content should be about THEM, nurture adds YOU into their lives, and conversion is where you earn the right to talk about yourself
opportunities: AI agent funnel scraper - a tool that automatically maps email sequences, landing pages, and conversion flows from competitors into a searchable database
Pay-what-you-want pricing model for SaaS products, following Sublime app's innovative approach
quotes: The best way to learn how to sell is studying how you got sold

episode_key: 2024-11-01 - Fire your team and hire AI employees?
title: Fire your team and hire AI employees?
executive_summary: Flo, CEO of Lindy.ai, demonstrates live how to build AI employees that handle meeting recording, email triage, scheduling, proposal writing, and design recruiting - all through natural language prompts. The key differentiator from Zapier is intelligence: Lindy agents maintain context across long conversations, understand nuance, and can delegate tasks to other agents. The episode showcases multi-agent orchestration where one Lindy delegates proposal writing to another, previewing the future of autonomous AI workforces.
key_takeaways: Start by mapping your 'jobs to be done' - identify every repetitive task in your business, then create individual AI agents (Lindies) for each before connecting them together
AI agents differ from automations (Zapier) in two critical ways: AI prompts can be used at any step, and agents manage long-term tasks with full conversational context
The business of the future is one person with an army of 2,000+ AI agents that scale elastically with demand - paying nothing when idle, scaling instantly during peaks
opportunities: Build a recruiting company powered entirely by AI agents - one person managing an army of AI recruiters that find candidates, verify emails, and send personalized outreach
AI-powered customer support that scales elastically for seasonal businesses (e.g., Black Friday spikes) without hiring temporary staff
quotes: The business of the future is basically you can set up a team of 5,000 AI employees in a weekend

episode_key: 2024-11-04 - 7 $1M+ AI startup ideas you can launch tomorrow with $0
title: 7 $1M+ AI startup ideas you can launch tomorrow with $0
executive_summary: Cody Schneider presents seven validated AI startup ideas with distribution strategies, emphasizing that AI enables 10x ad creative volume at 1/100th the cost. Key ideas include AI UGC ad generation (replacing $500/ad with $0.99 AI avatars), AI infographic generators with exact-match domains, AI journaling apps with high retention, and AI receptionists for vertical markets like dentists. The throughline: don't invent new categories - take existing behaviors people already pay for and make them 10x faster/cheaper with AI.
key_takeaways: Run A-to-Z testing instead of A/B testing - create 100+ ad variations simultaneously using AI-generated UGC to identify winning creatives at 1/100th traditional cost

Prioritize startup ideas by usage frequency - daily/weekly cadence apps (journaling, infographics) dramatically outperform one-time-use tools (logo generators, business plan writers) in retention and revenue
AI receptionists for vertical markets (dentists, barbershops) represent a massive opportunity - labor shortages make businesses receptive, and APIs like Vapi make building them accessible
opportunities: AI UGC ad agency - produce unlimited AI-avatar content variations for e-commerce brands at a fraction of traditional UGC creator costs
AI receptionist SaaS for specific verticals (medical, dental, restaurants) - build a wrapper around voice APIs and sell directly to local businesses
quotes: Don't be creative - find a category that has existed forever and will exist forever, then remix it with new technology

episode_key: 2024-11-06 - 24 year old making $1m/month from AI shares 3 startup ideas
title: 24 year old making $1m/month from AI shares 3 startup ideas
executive_summary: Blake Anderson (23), founder of Cal AI ($1M+/month in revenue) and Umax (10M+ downloads), shares three AI app ideas: Career AI (all-in-one job advancement platform), Journal AI (emotional support journaling with memory), and a Duolingo competitor using real-time AI tutoring. His framework: find stagnant industries, add AI to make the experience 10x better, then distribute via TikTok with highly iterative influencer marketing. Critical insight: build personal relationships with creators rather than using marketplace platforms.
key_takeaways: Split-test hooks across multiple creator niches simultaneously - assess views AND conversion per view to build a statistical model for future distribution decisions

Develop personal relationships with influencers (DM every platform, message their family if needed) rather than relying on marketplace databases - relationship-driven outreach dramatically outperforms cold approaches
Optimize for users^N (N>1) x profit-per-user rather than users x profit - accepting lower LTV per download in exchange for faster market dominance and unmeasurable word-of-mouth growth
opportunities: Career AI - an all-in-one platform combining resume analysis, career quizzes, and job application tools with gamified scoring (like Umax's 6-factor rating) for viral TikTok distribution
Language learning AI app - capture even 1% of Duolingo's market by iterating on AI features faster than incumbents whose engineers lack aligned incentives
quotes: Sometimes the most obvious ideas are the best ideas

episode_key: 2024-11-13 - How to build a $1M+ Consumer SaaS App (step-by-step guide)
title: How to build a $1M+ Consumer SaaS App (step-by-step guide)
executive_summary: Joseph Choy breaks down how consumer apps are generating millions through TikTok organic distribution, revealing that e-commerce TikTok Shop tactics are being adapted for app marketing. He demonstrates how to find TikTok Shop creators (already experts at converting views to sales) and redirect them to promote apps instead of physical products. Key app ideas include golf swing analysis AI, poker coaching AI, and skincare analysis - all targeting high-LTV demographics through low-cost content creation with AI-generated slideshows.
key_takeaways: Hire TikTok creators as actors (not influencers) on brand-new accounts - the algorithm serves content to the right audience regardless of follower count, so zero-follower accounts work fine
Target high-LTV demographics often overlooked on TikTok (golfers, poker players) where there's less creator competition but high willingness to pay for apps
Build apps that serve as utilities within existing TikTok Shop creator content - e.g., a skincare analysis app that helps beauty creators demonstrate product results, giving you free distribution
opportunities: Golf swing analysis app using GPT Vision - $70K/month revenue potential with 10K downloads targeting a demographic willing to pay $125 for in-person alternatives
Capitalize on cult movements (looksmaxing, wealth-building) by creating the definitive app for that community with built-in viral shareability
quotes: TikTok organic is probably the biggest underpriced distribution channel right now

episode_key: 2024-11-18 - 3 $1M+ AI startup ideas to build in 2025 (and how to grow them)
title: 3 $1M+ AI startup ideas to build in 2025 (and how to grow them)
executive_summary: Arvid Kahl, indie hacker and founder of PodScan, presents three AI-agent business ideas: an AI co-founder that combines marketing, sales, and strategy roles with personality training; a silent code refactoring service that continuously optimizes codebases (potential Sentry competitor); and a niche-specific data aggregation platform that summarizes industry news in personalized formats. The key framework: AI agents should start narrow (one niche, one role) and expand, not try to serve all founders at once.
key_takeaways: An AI co-founder service should start with one specific founder type in one niche (e.g., YC SaaS founders) - training models for specific domains is expensive, so narrow focus enables better quality
Silent code refactoring as a service would continuously scan, optimize, and test code improvements in sandboxed environments - start at $50/month for linting/error tracking and scale to enterprise
Build niche data aggregation businesses at the intersection of your existing expertise - a horse person building horse industry intelligence, for example, can speak the language and build trust
opportunities: AI co-founder SaaS with Mastermind-style agent interactions - multiple AI agents representing different expertise areas could cross-pollinate ideas between solo founders' businesses

Niche industry intelligence newsletters with per-subscriber personalization - deliver the same source material as bullet points, long-form email, AI podcast, or video depending on each reader's preference
quotes: If you start a business today, you can't avoid AI - you should never avoid it

episode_key: 2024-11-25 - 3 startup ideas from the co-founder of Loom (make $120k/mo)
title: 3 startup ideas from the co-founder of Loom (make $120k/mo)
executive_summary: Shahid, co-founder of Loom (sold for ~$1B), shares three ideas born from personal frustration: an anti-TripAdvisor travel guide app curating hidden gems from TikTok micro-influencers; a 'Path for 2025' micro social network limited to 150 close friends showing real-time locations (Dunbar's number); and ephemeral time capsule gifts that package shared photo memories for physical delivery. The unifying thesis: millennials crave curation, intimacy, and nostalgia - all underserved by current platforms.
key_takeaways: Use TikTok Creator Search Insights to find content gaps and trending travel searches - build city-specific coffee/bookstore guides as directories monetized at $0.99 per unlocked guide
Clone the best elements of Path (2013 social network, sold for ~$100M) for today's market - limit to 150 users, charge $20-30/year, and leverage PR around anti-social-media positioning
Nostalgia is a monetizable emotion - shared albums are where memories go to die, creating an opportunity for curated physical time capsule gifts paired with ephemeral digital experiences
opportunities: Anti-TripAdvisor travel directory starting with one category (e.g., coffee shops) in one city, using TikTok API + Descript transcription + AI to auto-generate SEO-friendly guides
Dunbar social network - a private, location-sharing micro social network capped at 150 friends, monetized through subscriptions and anonymized data partnerships
quotes: Shared albums are where your memories go to die

episode_key: 2024-11-27 - How This Guy Makes His Entire Year's Revenue in Black Friday WEEK
title: How This Guy Makes His Entire Year's Revenue in Black Friday WEEK
executive_summary: Jonathan Courtney dissects elite Black Friday strategies being used by brands like Apple, AG1, Magic Mind, and Four Sigmatic, revealing that a well-executed campaign week can generate an entire year's revenue. Key tactics include: Apple's $200 gift voucher approach (discount without cheapening the brand), escalating MIFGE gifts throughout the week, store credit framing ($21.63 credit vs. 20% off), and Four Sigmatic's gamified build-your-own-bundle experience. The biggest missed opportunity: SaaS companies ignoring seasonal campaigns entirely.
key_takeaways: Frame discounts as store credit with specific odd amounts ($21.63 instead of 20% off) to create urgency and perceived ownership - this dramatically outperforms standard percentage discounts
Start planning Black Friday campaigns in June, not November - escalate free gifts throughout the week, adding new bonuses for late buyers while honoring early purchasers
A dedicated Black Friday campaign agency is a viable business - reach out to e-commerce companies in August, take a revenue cut instead of a flat fee, and proactively build their entire campaign
opportunities: Black Friday campaign agency - proactively reach out to e-commerce brands offering to run their entire holiday campaign for a revenue share, no flat fees
Shopify app for gamified build-your-own-bundle experiences with progressive discount checkpoints (like Four Sigmatic's model)
quotes: If you can do a great Black Friday sale, you can literally mess up everything for the rest of the year and still have a great year

episode_key: 2024-12-02 - Build a high traffic startup using AI, Webflow and Zapier
title: Build a high traffic startup using AI, Webflow and Zapier
executive_summary: Omar demonstrates how he built two AI-powered directory sites - Guilty Chef (recipes) and Best Dubai (travel) - that compete with TripAdvisor and rank #1 on Google, all as side projects using Webflow, Zapier, Apify, and Claude. The technical walkthrough shows how to input a single Google Maps Place ID and auto-generate fully designed, schema-optimized pages in under a minute. Guilty Chef hit 100K monthly traffic in 7 months with zero ongoing effort; Best Dubai ranked 4th organically within 30 days.
key_takeaways: Use Google Maps Apify scrapers + Claude AI to auto-generate rich directory pages from a single Place ID input - the entire content pipeline (descriptions, scores, menus, FAQs) runs through Zapier
Schema markup is the secret weapon most directories miss - properly structured recipe/restaurant schema lets Google display your content as rich snippets, outranking sites with 90+ domain authority
Create embeddable rating widgets for listed businesses to earn backlinks organically - restaurants proudly display '97% score on BestDubai.com' badges, building your domain authority passively
opportunities: City-specific travel directory with AI-generated content, scoring algorithms, and menu scraping from delivery platforms - acquire exact-match domains (bestcity.com) while they're still affordable
AI-powered recipe directory competing with Trip Advisor by generating schema-optimized pages from restaurant menus scraped via Chrome extensions
quotes: If you spend zero time on this and it sits there getting 100K monthly traffic, that's the power of AI-generated directories

episode_key: 2024-12-09 - 3 AI app ideas that are goldmines (and how to grow them)
title: 3 AI app ideas that are goldmines (and how to grow them)
executive_summary: Dan Shipper, who runs an AI incubation studio, presents three ideas: advanced voice notes that let recipients talk to your transcribed conversations; N-of-One, a Kaggle-like platform for personal data bounties (e.g., predicting OCD from biometrics); and AI-reimagined Great Books in modern, digestible multimedia formats. He also reveals TLDR, an internal tool that converts meetings into NotebookLM-style podcasts. The meta-insight: new technologies create opportunities for new media formats in places previously too expensive for storytelling.
key_takeaways: Look for places where stories need to be told but were previously too expensive - AI makes storytelling nearly free, opening massive new categories (meeting summaries, personalized education)
Kaggle.com has 406K public datasets that anyone can now analyze with GPT-4 - browse datasets to generate startup ideas around data-as-a-service businesses
Charge per successfully completed task rather than monthly subscriptions - consumers are fatigued by subscriptions, and usage-based pricing aligns value with payment
opportunities: Interactive voice notes platform where recipients can ask follow-up questions to an AI trained on the conversation transcript
Personal data bounty marketplace - post biometric/health datasets with cash prizes for anyone who can build useful predictive models
quotes: New technologies create the opportunity for new media formats

episode_key: 2024-12-11 - Inside the Brain of a 17-Year-Old $1M/Month AI Founder
title: Inside the Brain of a 17-Year-Old $1M/Month AI Founder
executive_summary: Zach Yagari (17), co-founder of Cal AI ($1M+/month), live-designs a 'Dr. AI' skin diagnosis app from scratch, walking through UX wireframes, feature prioritization, prompting strategy, and monetization via paywalls. His framework: identify 2-3 core AI features (scan, symptom quiz, chatbot), design for digestibility over beauty, and build a history feed that creates switching costs. Distribution would rely on paid ads and influencer marketing playing on health anxiety emotions.
key_takeaways: Design consumer AI apps around 'primitives' - reusable UX patterns like scanning screens, rating systems, and chat interfaces that users already understand from other apps
Never force users to wait on a loading screen - return them to the home feed with a loading indicator so they don't feel trapped during 30-60 second API calls
Build history/log features into utility apps to create switching costs and retention - users won't leave if all their past scans, chats, and results live in your app
opportunities: Dr. AI - skin condition analysis app using GPT Vision with danger level scoring, symptom quizzes, and follow-up chat, monetized through freemium paywalls
Cal AI for X template - apply the scan + score + chat primitive pattern to any category (fitness form analysis, plant disease detection, home inspection)
quotes: If you aren't even feeling a little bit embarrassed by your first version, you probably spent too long on it

episode_key: 2024-12-18 - The framework for building AI startups from a product design genius
title: The framework for building AI startups from a product design genius
executive_summary: Mike Hudack (ex-Facebook, founder of Sling Money) shares his framework for building AI agent startups: single clear action, instant feedback, bounded decisions, and zero friction. He advocates that agencies are the perfect template for AI agents - find any manual agency workflow and automate it. The episode showcases tools like Greenlight (fin-crime compliance AI), Granola (collaborative AI note-taking), and Bland (AI phone calls), revealing that the best AI products solve specific, manual, repetitive tasks within tight feedback loops.
key_takeaways: Build AI agents following the SIBL framework: Single action, Instant feedback, Bounded decisions, zero friction - then expand slowly from there
Agencies are the new template for AI agents - buy or partner with an existing agency, learn their manual processes, then automate them with AI
B2C has higher dynamic range (bigger wins, bigger failures) while B2B has tighter, more predictable outcomes - choose based on your risk tolerance and personality type
opportunities: Take successful US AI products (like Bland for phone calls) and build the geo-specific version for other countries - Simple (Canadian Robinhood) is a $3.5B company proving geography-specific plays work
AI debt collection agents that outperform humans through consistent empathy and thoughtful negotiation scripts
quotes: We don't make movies to make money, we make money to make more movies

episode_key: 2024-12-20 - How to get 145k followers on LinkedIn in 1 year
title: How to get 145k followers on LinkedIn in 1 year
executive_summary: Greg Isenberg reveals how he grew from 0 to 145K LinkedIn followers in 14 months with roughly 15 hours of total work, generating millions in revenue. The tactical playbook: post videos to LinkedIn's new video tab (fishing with dynamite), pair personal stories with selfies, reshare viral infographics with agreeable one-liners, and critically - only add links AFTER posts hit 150+ likes (per LinkedIn's own team). He also details his ACP funnel: build Audience, convert to Community, then build Products.
key_takeaways: LinkedIn suppresses posts with links - edit your post to add links only AFTER it exceeds 150-200 likes (4+ hours post-publish), per advice from LinkedIn's own team
Three content types that crush on LinkedIn: videos (LinkedIn is becoming TikTok), vulnerable personal stories paired with selfies, and reshared viral infographics with agreeable captions
Create multiple lead magnets monthly segmented by interest - 5,000 people downloaded a startup ideas database in 30 days, creating a segmented email list ready for future monetization
opportunities: LinkedIn growth agency - most professionals don't know about the video tab, link suppression workaround, or story-with-selfie format, creating a service opportunity
ACP funnel businesses: start with an audience (Twitter/LinkedIn account), convert to community, then build product - boring marketing.com grew to $7-8M ARR from a Twitter account
quotes: 2024 LinkedIn is the equivalent of 2020 Twitter - it's fishing with dynamite

episode_key: 2025-01-01 - 10 (Profitable) Newsletter Business Ideas To Start In 2025
title: 10 (Profitable) Newsletter Business Ideas To Start In 2025
executive_summary: Greg Isenberg analyzes Matt McGary's list of 10 newsletter business ideas, adding his own monetization insights. Top picks include: platform-specific newsletters (become the Miss Excel for any tool, earn 20-50% affiliate commissions), local real estate investor newsletters ($190K/year possible with just 5-10K subs through events + premium subs + ads), and B2B decision-maker newsletters (2,000 C-suite subscribers can generate more revenue than 100K general subs). The meta-lesson: newsletters are startup cheat codes because they build segmented, monetizable audiences before you need a product.
key_takeaways: You don't need 100K subscribers to make $100K/year - 5-10K highly-targeted subscribers (local real estate investors, C-suite executives) can generate $190K+ through events, premium tiers, and targeted sponsorships
Collect subscriber data at signup (job title, company, skills) not just email - this data multiplies your sponsorship revenue and enables targeted campaigns worth $1-3K per blast
Use pre-CTAs and post-CTAs on social media to drive newsletter signups - tease upcoming content ('drops tomorrow in my newsletter') rather than sharing links directly in posts
opportunities: Platform-specific newsletter (Ableton, DaVinci, Notion) earning 20-50% recurring affiliate commissions plus template sales - platforms may eventually acquire you
Local real estate investor newsletter + $500/seat quarterly seminar events in cities with 200K-1M population, scaling horizontally to neighboring cities
quotes: Newsletters are a cheat code - you build a loved group of people and can build a marketplace, agency, or SaaS on top

episode_key: 2025-01-07 - Q&A How to get 100,000 followers in 2025
title: Q&A How to get 100,000 followers in 2025
executive_summary: Tyler Lemco (founding team of Epic Meal Time, 600M+ views) interviews Greg Isenberg about transitioning from creative-for-hire to solopreneur. The episode becomes a live workshop on crafting social bios using the CIV framework (Credibility, Intrigue, Value proposition) and discusses how AI tools like Claude enhance but don't replace human creative collaboration. Key advice: crawl-walk-run into solopreneurship, build audience before product, and the goal of every solopreneur should be to eventually NOT be a solopreneur.
key_takeaways: Craft social bios using the CIV framework: Credibility (billion+ views generated), Intrigue (unexpected angle), Value proposition (what followers get) - bios convert non-followers, not existing ones
Use AI (Claude/ChatGPT) to clarify ideas BEFORE jamming with humans - it serves the same function as writing: forcing you to articulate half-formed thoughts
The goal of every solopreneur should be to not remain a solopreneur - start alone to validate, but plan from day one to bring on people who complement your weaknesses
opportunities: Creativity-as-wellness community - positioned like Calm for meditation but focused on creative practice as a path to happiness, targeting the growing anti-screen-time movement
Content consulting for creators transitioning from hired work to building their own brands
quotes: The goal of every solopreneur should be to not be a solopreneur

episode_key: 2025-01-30 - ChatGPT Operator Built a $500/Day Business in 30 Minutes
title: ChatGPT Operator Built a $500/Day Business in 30 Minutes
executive_summary: Chris Corner demonstrates ChatGPT's Operator agent live, showing three business applications: retail arbitrage (finding undervalued pizza ovens on Facebook Marketplace for resale on eBay), automated vendor outreach (submitting catering quote requests for a fake wedding), and research automation. The agent browses the web autonomously, fills forms, messages sellers, and logs findings in spreadsheets. Key insight: Operator is buggy but functional - the arbitrage is in mastering the prompting while others dismiss it as broken.
key_takeaways: Use ChatGPT Operator for retail arbitrage - have it scan Facebook Marketplace for undervalued items, cross-reference eBay prices, message sellers, and log everything in a spreadsheet automatically
Tell Operator to 'move faster' and 'don't ask before submitting' to dramatically improve speed - simple prompt tweaks create significant performance differences
The biggest opportunity isn't the tool itself but the prompting knowledge gap - early adopters who master prompting will build businesses while critics complain about bugs
opportunities: Automated retail arbitrage business using AI agents to find price discrepancies across marketplaces and negotiate purchases - potentially six or seven figures
NeverCallAgain.com - a service using voice AI agents to make 100 simultaneous calls for quotes (catering, venues, contractors), returning results in minutes
quotes: We have artificial intelligence working for us - calm down

episode_key: 2025-02-03 - 4 microSaaS ideas you can build to make $100k/month
title: 4 microSaaS ideas you can build to make $100k/month
executive_summary: Rafi, founder of Sublime, presents four ideas rooted in the question 'what becomes scarce in an AI world?' Top ideas: a franchise model for parental screen-time consultants (doctorscreentime.com, $2K/reset), a 'business in a box' for craft/art workshops (the anti-AI leisure trend), Lambda School for tradespeople, and a podcast insight capture tool using screenshots + OCR. The philosophical thread: in an AI-dominated future, physical skills, human touch, and analog experiences become the new luxury and status symbols.
key_takeaways: What becomes scarce in an AI world is the key strategic question - physical craftsmanship, hands-on skills, and offline experiences will gain status as white-collar jobs get automated
Build 'business in a box' franchise models (like Zumba's 15K licensed instructors) rather than traditional SaaS - arm people with a brand, training, and customer acquisition tools for a monthly fee
The Sony Walkman lesson: resist adding features (the 'record button') - build AI tools that do ONE thing exceptionally well rather than trying to be an AI co-founder for everything
opportunities: DoctorScreenTime.com - franchise model where licensed consultants do in-home $2K family screen-time resets, monetized through affiliate recommendations for parental control products
Lambda School for trades - zero-tuition training for plumbers/electricians with income-share agreements, paired with AI-powered customer acquisition for graduates
quotes: Offline is the new luxury

episode_key: 2025-03-10 - 3 Proven startup trends that will make $124,000/Month
title: 3 Proven startup trends that will make $124,000/Month
executive_summary: Greg and Cody Schneider explore three trending opportunities: a Buy It For Life product directory (69K monthly searches, affiliate + display ad monetization), exact-match domain SEO plays like jobdescriptiongenerator.com (buy domains for $800, build niche tools that generate branded searches), and AI landing page builders for rapid A/B testing. The tactical playbook: find trending subreddits, validate with Google Trends/Glimpse, buy exact-match domains, build directories with AI, drive initial traffic from Reddit, then scale via SEO and newsletters.
key_takeaways: Buy exact-match domains for trending categories ($800-$1,200) and build niche directories/tools - branded search traffic signals to Google that you're a real company, boosting rankings across longtail keywords
Competitors validate your idea, they don't kill it - if someone already built buylife.com, that proves market demand; there's room for multiple winners in any 70K+ monthly search category
Drive initial Reddit traffic by posting value-first content and simply signing off with your name + domain - don't explicitly promote, let curiosity drive organic clicks
opportunities: Buy It For Life directory with sale alert notifications and affiliate links - start as a newsletter, evolve into a platform with price-tracking across major retailers
Exact-match domain tool play - buy domains like jobdescriptiongenerator.com, build a free tool as homepage, then create 10K+ longtail blog pages for SEO dominance
quotes: People get so worried about competitors in internet businesses - there's 50 window cleaning companies in your city and all of them make money

episode_key: 2025-03-21 - He makes $750 a day 'Vibe Coding' Apps (using Replit, ChatGPT, Upwork)
title: He makes $750 a day 'Vibe Coding' Apps (using Replit, ChatGPT, Upwork)
executive_summary: Billy Howell demonstrates how to earn $750+/day by finding clients on Upwork who need custom apps built on platforms like Airtable, then delivering superior solutions via Replit vibe coding. The process: search Upwork for jobs referencing specific SaaS tools, prototype a replacement app in Replit in under an hour, send a Loom demo as the proposal, and charge $750-$2,500+ per project with recurring hosting fees. Non-developers can do this - Billy studied public policy, not CS.
key_takeaways: Find buyers BEFORE you build - search Upwork for jobs mentioning specific tools (Airtable, HubSpot, Google Sheets), then build a better prototype in Replit and send a Loom demo as your proposal
Feed Upwork job descriptions into ChatGPT to generate a PRD (Product Requirements Document), then paste that PRD into Replit to one-shot a functional MVP in minutes
Focus on CRUD apps (create, read, update, delete data) - these are simple enough for vibe coding to handle reliably, and avoid complex integrations like payments or DocuSign initially
opportunities: Vibe coding freelance business replacing expensive SaaS subscriptions ($20/seat/month) with custom Replit apps at $750-$2,500 per project plus recurring hosting fees
Systematize the Upwork-to-Replit pipeline to build a micro-agency serving specific verticals (nonprofits, tutoring businesses, local services) with templated solutions
quotes: Most people get this wrong - they build something and then try to sell it. Find someone who wants to buy, then build it.

episode_key: 2025-03-31 - Build $1000/day cash-flowing startup on beehiiv (here's how)
title: Build $1000/day cash-flowing startup on beehiiv (here's how)
executive_summary: This episode provides a tactical blueprint for building a $1,000/day newsletter business using beehiiv, covering subscriber acquisition through paid ads and organic content, monetization via sponsorships and premium subscriptions, and scaling strategies. The focus is on choosing high-value niches where small subscriber counts (5-10K) can generate outsized revenue through targeted sponsorships and digital product sales.
key_takeaways: Choose newsletter niches where subscribers have high purchasing power - B2B decision-makers and investors generate 10-50x more revenue per subscriber than general audiences
Use beehiiv's built-in monetization tools including ad network, premium subscriptions, and referral programs to create multiple revenue streams from day one
Reinvest early ad revenue into subscriber acquisition to create a growth flywheel - target $2 CPA or less on Facebook/Meta ads
opportunities: Niche newsletter businesses generating $1,000+/day through a combination of sponsorships, premium content, and digital product sales on beehiiv
Newsletter-first startup approach where the audience becomes the foundation for future SaaS, community, or marketplace products
quotes: A newsletter is the easiest way to start a startup - you build the audience first, then figure out what to sell them

episode_key: 2025-04-02 - ChatGPT 4o replaces your $10,000 Ad Agency (Full Tutorial)
title: ChatGPT 4o replaces your $10,000 Ad Agency (Full Tutorial)
executive_summary: A tutorial demonstrating how ChatGPT 4o can replace a $10,000/month ad agency by generating ad copy, creative concepts, audience targeting strategies, and full campaign structures. The episode walks through prompting techniques for creating high-converting Facebook, Google, and TikTok ad campaigns, including A/B test variants and landing page copy - all for the cost of a ChatGPT subscription.
key_takeaways: Use ChatGPT 4o to generate complete ad campaigns including copy, targeting, creative briefs, and A/B test variants - replacing $10K/month agency fees

episode_key: 2025-08-07 - Making $$$ with Sam Altman's Solopreneurship Thesis with GPT5
title: Making $$$ with Sam Altman's Solopreneurship Thesis with GPT5
executive_summary: An exploration of Sam Altman's thesis that GPT-5 and beyond will enable solopreneurs to build billion-dollar companies, and what that means for builders today. The episode discusses how to position yourself for the GPT-5 era by building distribution, brand, and domain expertise now while AI capabilities catch up to enable full automation of operations, development, and customer service.
key_takeaways: Sam Altman's solopreneurship thesis means building audience and distribution NOW while waiting for AI capabilities to enable one-person billion-dollar companies
Focus on accumulating three types of capital: distribution (audience), domain expertise (industry knowledge), and relationships (network) - these are the assets AI can't replace
Start building AI-first workflows today so you're ready to scale when next-generation models unlock new capabilities
opportunities: Position yourself as the solopreneur expert in a specific vertical - when AI enables one-person companies, domain experts with distribution will have the strongest competitive advantage
Build AI automation infrastructure for your business now so you can scale instantly when more powerful models become available
quotes: The solopreneurs who build distribution today will be the ones running billion-dollar AI companies tomorrow

episode_key: 2025-08-11 - How to Build Your Entire AI Workforce in One Afternoon (Live Demo)
title: How to Build Your Entire AI Workforce in One Afternoon (Live Demo)
executive_summary: A live demonstration of building an entire AI workforce in one afternoon - covering AI agents for sales, marketing, customer support, operations, and finance. The episode shows how to set up interconnected AI agents that handle real business functions, communicate with each other, and escalate to humans only when necessary. The result: a functioning AI team that operates 24/7 at a fraction of human labor costs.
key_takeaways: An AI workforce can be built in an afternoon using no-code tools - start with customer-facing roles (support, sales) then expand to internal operations
Design AI agent handoffs carefully - define clear escalation criteria so agents know when to involve humans versus handling autonomously
The ROI of an AI workforce compounds over time as agents learn from interactions and improve their performance continuously
opportunities: AI workforce setup consultancy for small businesses wanting to automate key functions without hiring
AI workforce management platform that provides a dashboard for monitoring and optimizing multiple AI agents across business functions
quotes: You can build your entire AI workforce in one afternoon - and it works 24/7 without vacation or sick days

episode_key: 2025-08-13 - The Most Overlooked $12.5K/mo AI Agent Business Anyone Can Start
title: The Most Overlooked $12.5K/mo AI Agent Business Anyone Can Start
executive_summary: The episode identifies overlooked AI agent business opportunities generating $12.5K+/month that most entrepreneurs are ignoring because they seem too simple or unsexy. The focus is on building AI agents for specific, boring business processes (invoice processing, appointment scheduling, data entry) where businesses are desperate for automation but the market isn't crowded with AI startups.
key_takeaways: The most profitable AI agent businesses solve boring, repetitive problems that nobody wants to build because they lack glamour - invoice processing, appointment scheduling, data entry
Target businesses that are currently paying humans $15-25/hour for repetitive tasks - offer AI agent replacement at a fraction of the cost with better consistency
Build vertical-specific AI agents rather than horizontal platforms - a dental appointment AI agent is more valuable than a generic scheduling bot
opportunities: AI agent business targeting a single boring process in a specific vertical - e.g., insurance claims processing, property management maintenance requests
AI agent marketplace connecting pre-built vertical agents with businesses ready to automate specific workflows
quotes: The most overlooked AI businesses are the boring ones - that's exactly why they're still available

episode_key: 2025-08-19 - Making $$$ with Voice AI agents and SaaS (my little secret)
title: Making $$$ with Voice AI agents and SaaS (my little secret)
executive_summary: A deep dive into the intersection of voice AI agents and SaaS, revealing how combining voice technology with recurring software subscriptions creates highly profitable businesses. The episode covers building voice AI agents that handle phone calls for specific industries, packaging them as SaaS products with monthly subscriptions, and the unit economics that make voice AI one of the most lucrative AI niches.
key_takeaways: Voice AI + SaaS = the best business model in AI right now - voice agents deliver immediate, measurable value (calls handled) that justifies monthly subscriptions
Industries with high call volumes and repetitive conversations (healthcare, restaurants, property management) are the ideal targets for voice AI SaaS
Price voice AI SaaS based on calls handled or appointments booked, not per-seat - usage-based pricing aligns value with revenue and scales naturally
opportunities: Voice AI SaaS for specific verticals (dental offices, restaurants, property management) combining agent technology with recurring subscription revenue
Voice AI agent reseller program enabling agencies to white-label and sell voice AI solutions to local businesses
quotes: Voice AI + SaaS is my little secret - it's the most profitable AI niche nobody's talking about

episode_key: 2025-08-21 - How Hormozi made $83m in 5 days (Ads, Emails, Pages, Everything)
title: How Hormozi made $83m in 5 days (Ads, Emails, Pages, Everything)
executive_summary: A comprehensive breakdown of Alex Hormozi's $83M launch that generated revenue in just 5 days, analyzing every element: the ad creative strategy, email sequence architecture, landing page design, pricing psychology, urgency mechanics, and cross-platform distribution. The episode extracts replicable lessons for entrepreneurs at any scale, showing how Hormozi's launch playbook can be adapted for smaller businesses.
key_takeaways: Hormozi's $83M launch combined massive pre-launch audience building, perfectly sequenced email campaigns, and irresistible offer stacking - the revenue was earned over months but collected in days
The launch playbook is scalable: build anticipation through content, create urgency through time-limited bonuses, and stack value until the price feels like a no-brainer at any level
Study the specific mechanics of world-class launches (email timing, ad creative rotation, landing page structure) and adapt them to your scale and audience
opportunities: Launch strategy consultancy applying Hormozi's playbook to smaller businesses and product launches
AI-powered launch automation tool that orchestrates email sequences, ad rotations, and landing page variants based on proven launch frameworks
quotes: Hormozi made $83M in 5 days - but the real lesson is the months of audience building and offer engineering that made it possible
